metric_value: After a period of decline, mainframe business is recovering
New generation mainframes: dramatically lower priced, less bulky, no plumbing required, substantially less electricity
notes: CMOS technology transformation of mainframe form factor and economics

metric_value: New mainframes can run UNIX and Windows NT applications in addition to legacy applications
notes: OpenEdition MVS and VM-based multi-OS hosting

metric_name: Mainframe clustering vs. UNIX server farms
metric_value: New clustering technology offers cost-effective alternative to large UNIX server farms
notes: Core competitive argument for mainframe revival

metric_value: Leading contender in mainframe market; G3/G4 represents modernized competitive enterprise solution
notes: IBM S/390 G3/G4 as Aberdeen's primary mainframe evaluation subject

metric_value: Primary IBM-compatible mainframe alternative; competitive pricing against IBM; focused on existing IBM mainframe customer base
notes: Amdahl as number-two mainframe vendor in 1996

metric_name: Amdahl viability as IBM mainframe alternative
metric_value: Viable alternative mainframe vendor with Millennium series; competitive with IBM S/390
notes: Implicit in study's treatment of Amdahl as leading contender

metric_value: Amdahl Corporation dissolved in 2000; ceased mainframe operations; Fujitsu absorbed remaining assets
notes: IBM's pricing aggression and technology lead made IBM-compatible mainframe competition unviable

metric_value: Large UNIX server farms require proportionally larger system administration staffs; management complexity underestimated
notes: Key TCO argument favoring mainframe consolidation

metric_value: Mainframes handle workload peaks more efficiently through dynamic resource allocation within single system image
Mainframe availability metrics (five-nines and above) remain superior to clustered UNIX alternatives
Enterprises with substantial COBOL and IMS/DB2 workloads find mainframe retention more economical than migration
notes: Third mainframe TCO advantage dimension; still valid in 2026

metric_value: Aberdeen assessment: mainframe revival is substantive rather than temporary; mainframe will occupy defensible tier in enterprise computing
notes: Core study thesis: long-term not short-lived

metric_value: IBM mainframe line evolved through zSeries (2000), System z9/z10 (2005-2008), z13/z14/z15/z16 (2015-2022); still dominant for OLTP/batch in 2026; IBM Z generates billions in annual revenue
notes: Prediction fully confirmed; IBM mainframe achieved multi-decade longevity

metric_value: CMOS processors replacing bipolar: dramatically reduced cost and power consumption; foundational to mainframe price/performance competitiveness
Study does not predict complete displacement of UNIX/NT servers; mainframe positioned for high-volume OLTP, batch, and mission-critical scenarios
